title: Stacking & z-index
description: How stacking contexts and z-index really decide who sits on top.

options: The element is a flex/grid child, OR positioned (not static)
questions.explanation: z-index works on positioned elements AND flex/grid items.

questions.prompt: What creates a new STACKING CONTEXT?
options: opacity < 1, transform, filter, isolation: isolate, etc.
questions.explanation: Lots of things: opacity<1, transform, filter, will-change, isolation, positioned + z-index, etc.
Higher z-index wins within the same stacking context.

questions.prompt: A high z-index inside a low-parent stacking context can:
options: Only compete inside its own context
questions.explanation: Children can't outrank a sibling of their PARENT stacking context.

questions.prompt: What is isolation: isolate used for?
options: Creates a fresh stacking context without other side effects
Isolates the element from the DOM
questions.explanation: Handy to trap z-index without adding transform or opacity.

options: auto — participates in parent context, no new context
questions.explanation: auto ≠ 0. It means "no new context, treat as 0 for ordering."
